summary: Large gmresultplayer decompilation PR that replaced many placeholder prototypes and raw data assumptions with C implementations, typed structs, bitfields, and corrected symbols. The decomp.dev report showed +1772 matched bytes overall, 5 new function matches in main/melee/gm/gmresultplayer, and 14 improvements, with one accepted regression. The main review note explicitly accepted the regression because the PR cleaned up pointer math and left mostly regswaps.
what_changed: Implemented a large block of gmresultplayer.c functionality, adding concrete functions such as fn_80178050, fn_801785B0, fn_80178BB4, fn_801791E4, fn_801795D4, fn_801796F0, fn_80179854, fn_8017A078, fn_8017A318, fn_8017A67C, and fn_8017AA78.
Updated gmresultplayer.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures for multiple functions, including HSD_GObj*, bool, int, s32, CharacterKind, and Fighter_GObj* return/argument types.

Added many required includes for HSD animation/object/camera/display types and gameplay subsystems used by the results-player code, including baselib aobj/cobj/dobj/jobj/mobj/random and melee ef, ftdemo, gm_1601, gm_1A45, ground, stage, item, archive, audio, bgflash, menu, collision, and player headers.

Introduced local typed views over data blobs in gmresultplayer.c, including CharScaleEntry, ResultsRenderFuncs, ResultsPlayerConfig, CameraKindParams, CameraKindData, lbl_8046E3AC_t, and ResultsDisplayData.
Changed ResultsPlayerData in types.h from a raw u8 x0 flags byte to named bitfields x0_0 through x0_4 plus x0_567, with x0_0 documented as the confirmed flag.
Changed ResultsData in types.h by grouping x0_2/x0_3 into x0_23 as a 2-bit phase field documented as idle/init/active, and exposed byte x3 instead of a padding byte.
Updated gmresult.c and gmresult.h so fn_80174A60 returns s32 instead of u8 and uses an s32 count local.
Rewrote gmresult.c flag manipulation in fn_80174B4C from raw masks on pdata->x0 to named bitfield assignments x0_1, x0_2, x0_3, and x0_4.

Adjusted GALE01 symbols so lbl_803D6A18 was split into a 0x10 object plus new lbl_803D6A28 of size 0x5F0, and collapsed multiple adjacent sdata labels lbl_804D3FD4-lbl_804D3FF0 into a single 0x20 object.
The bot report recorded 5 new full matches: fn_801791E4, fn_801796F0, fn_801795D4, fn_80179F84, and fn_80177DD0.
smart_moves: Replacing raw flag masks with bitfields made cross-file results logic clearer while preserving matching constraints.
Correcting fn_80174A60 from u8 to s32 aligned the helper with its count local and downstream arithmetic, reducing type friction in callers.
Creating narrow local structs for known data layouts let the PR remove pointer math without prematurely requiring globally polished names.
The symbols.txt edits treated adjacent anonymous data as structured objects where the new C code needed array-like access, avoiding a mismatch between code assumptions and symbol boundaries.
The author annotated a suspicious cast in fn_80177DD0 as permuter slop, documenting that the unsigned long long slot cast was a matching artifact rather than semantic intent.
The PR accepted a small matching regression in one unmatched item to unlock much larger readability and matching improvements elsewhere.
decomp_lessons: For results-screen work, converting a flags byte into ordered bitfields can enable cleaner source and improve downstream decomp without changing layout.
When a function is used in arithmetic, verify return width against actual generated code; an overly narrow return type such as u8 can force awkward mismatches.
Large UI/state data blobs can often be advanced by introducing offset-commented partial structs with padding, arrays, and known terminal fields instead of leaving everything as byte pointers.
Symbol boundaries matter: if C code treats adjacent data as a table, symbols.txt may need to split or merge labels to reflect the actual object layout.
Document permuter-only casts or stack padding so future cleanup knows what is semantic and what is only present for matching.
A small regression can be acceptable when it replaces inscrutable pointer math with structured access and leaves remaining mismatch mostly as register swaps, but that tradeoff should be explicit.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions remain address-based fn_801xxxxx names until semantic names are known.
Partial structs use offset-style field names such as x20, x24, x30, x3C, x6, x22F4, and pad_00/pad_000 when semantics are not yet known.

Typed helper structs use descriptive interim names when evidence is strong enough, such as CharScaleEntry, ResultsRenderFuncs, ResultsPlayerConfig, CameraKindParams, CameraKindData, ResultsDisplayData, and ResultsPlayerData.
Bitfields retain x0_N-style names when semantics are incomplete, with comments added for known meanings such as confirmed flag and phase.
Function declarations keep address comments such as /* 178050 */ beside prototypes in headers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in fn_80177B7C and PAD_STACK(16) in fn_80178050 to satisfy stack layout requirements.
Used an explicitly noted unsigned long long cast in a fn_801748EC call from fn_80177DD0 as permuter slop.
Modeled contiguous sdata entries as a static s32 lbl_804D3FD0[4][2] table, supported by collapsing symbols.txt entries into a single 0x20 object.
Converted raw bitmask OR/AND operations into equivalent bitfield assignments in fn_80174B4C.
Used offset-commented structs and arrays to express data access patterns while keeping unknown fields padded.
Kept some C shaped for matching, including explicit casts, do-while loops, and temporary variables visible in the diff.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.body: Regression is fine since it cleaned up a lot of pointer math and most of the remaining bits are regswaps.
Matched code increased by +0.05% / +1772 bytes, with 5 new matches and 14 improvements in unmatched gmresultplayer items; one unmatched item, fn_8017A9B4, regressed by 72 bytes from 94.27% to 57.18%.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that semantically rename gmresultplayer fn_80178050 or related results-screen functions.
Search for future cleanup of ResultsPlayerData bitfields x0_1 through x0_4 and ResultsData phase field x0_23.
Check whether lbl_804D3FD0, lbl_803D6A18, lbl_803D6A28, or CameraKindData receive final names or global struct definitions.
Look for follow-up PRs resolving the accepted fn_8017A9B4 regression or remaining gmresultplayer regswaps.
